person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
His coat of arms were: "or two bars gules" (c 1285, St. George's Roll).

"He succeeded to the bulk of his father's estate, doing homage to the king for his manor of Stanton in April 1258. William made two marriages, the first (before 1253) to Alice, sister of Alan de la Zouche, which brought him the manor of Tong, Shropshire, and Braunstone, Leicestershire, from his father. The second marriage was to a certain Hilaria. He became involved with the Montfortian cause in 1264-?5, although there is little evidence to link him with the Montfort affinity in the midlands before then. His brother Sir Saher was, however, a long-term associate of Earl Simon, and was ruined by his involvement with the baronial cause. William was burdened with fines of 500 marks, but in 1267 found a group of neighbours and relatives able to guarantee and clear 400 marks, which the king had granted to Sir Philip Basset, and in 1269 negotiated the clearing of a further 100 marks owed to William de Valence. William died in 1270, leaving his only son, Sir Richard de Harcourt (1256-?1293), a minor, as heir. Richard had proof of age in 1277, when his date of birth was agreed as having been 9 December 1256 in Oxfordshire (presumably at Stanton), which would have made him the son of William's first marriage."

Sir William de Harcourt's second wife Hilary was daughter of Henry de Hastings. "In Oct. 1269 Joan, widow of Henry de Hastings, sued William de Harcourt and Hillary his wife for a third part of £20 of land in Nailstone, Leicestershire as her right of dower. Sir William de Harcourt died shortly before 5 July 1270."

«b»Children«/b»
«b»Child by first wife Alice de la Zouche:«/b»

1.) Orabel, married Henry de Pembridge.

«b»Child by second wife Hilary de Hastings:«/b»

1.) Richard (1256-1293); m. (1) Margaret Beke, m. (2) Joan, widow of John Fitz Walkelyn.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Harcourt於大約1227年出生在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰時，他的父親Sir Richard de Harcourt29歲，母親Orabella Quincy29歲。他於大約1248年與Alice la Zouche在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1271年4月19日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Harcourt was born about 1227, in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Sir Richard de Harcourt, was 29 and his mother, Orabella Quincy, was 29. He married Alice la Zouche about 1248, in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died before 19 April 1271, in his hometown, and was buried in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Alice la Zouche environ 1248, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 19 avril 1271, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Harcourtが約1227年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン スト ジョンで生まれた時，父Sir Richard de Harcourtは29歳，母Orabella Quincyは29歳であった。約1248年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアでAlice la Zoucheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1271年4月19日以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアスタントン ハーコウルトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Harcourt(이)가 대략 1227년 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard de Harcourt(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Orabella Quincy(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1248년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르에서 Alice la Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1271년 4월 19일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Harcourt родился около 1227 г. в местности стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard de Harcourt было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Orabella Quincy, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice la Zouche около 1248 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер перед 19 апрель 1271 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности стантон харкоурт, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Alice la Zouche aproximadamente 1248, en Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió antes de 19 de abril de 1271, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
